summary: 2018 winners and 2022 finalists, chasing a historic third consecutive World Cup final under Didier Deschamps (his fourth and final tournament, coach since 2012). Pragmatic but effective — 13 wins in 18 since the Euro 2024 semi-final, including victories over Brazil and Colombia — with unrivalled attacking depth.
style: 4-2-3-1, with Deschamps constantly shuffling his forwards around Kylian Mbappé at No 9 (Kolo Muani, Thuram, Ekitike fitted in wide). Physically imposing — threatens with back-post crosses and cuts teams apart on the counter. Can press high or sit deep, rotates positions, and likes one-twos in build-up.
strengths: Elite attacking depth with a flexible, rotating front line around Mbappé
Physical presence and back-post crossing plus lethal counter-attacks
Tactical flexibility — high press or deep block
Mike Maignan in outstanding form behind it all
weaknesses: Creativity/finishing questions linger from Euro 2024 (4 goals in 6, just one in open play)
Shaky run in the 2024-25 Nations League (5-1 down to Spain, recovered deficits vs Croatia)

keyPlayers.name: Kylian Mbappé
keyPlayers.note: The focal point — top scorer in qualifying (5); 56 in 97 caps, one short of equalling Giroud's France record

keyPlayers.name: Mike Maignan
keyPlayers.note: Career-best season — 14 clean sheets and the 3rd-best post-shot xG of any top-five-league keeper (~13 goals prevented above average)

keyPlayers.name: Ousmane Dembélé
keyPlayers.note: 2025 Ballon d'Or winner; kept wide to accommodate Mbappé, but tempting for his pressing and passing

tactics: Build around Mbappé at No 9, rotate the forwards, and break at pace from a structured base — pressing high or sitting deep as the game demands. The talent and defence are elite; the open question is whether the creativity and finishing have improved since Euro 2024.
notes: Only Brazil (1994-2002) and West Germany (1982-1990) have reached three straight World Cup finals.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group I (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
